title: ulmus scabra subf. viminalis
intro: Ulmus scabra subf. viminalis, also known as the Japanese elm or Viminalis elm, is a subspecies of Ulmus scabra, a deciduous tree native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever and inflammation, as well as its antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Ulmus scabra subf. viminalis include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant are often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
